text:
A small bird flutters up to a giant oak tree. The tree seems to laugh heartily.

The bird lands on an open branch.

"Yes?" asks the tree.

"You've got some wonderful seed pods over there," the bird says.

"Thank you."

It dives down and collects a juicy pod. It returns with a victorious song.

"Listen," it whispers. "Inside these are the sweetest fruits. I'll take you a ride."

The bird takes the pod in its beak, then swoops forth. Atop a tall windmill they circle.

"Try these," says the bird.

The windmill spins. The fruit shivers magically.

"Mmm," they murmur. "Delicious."

And so they nibbled on the delightful fruit. Not just once, but countless times. The windmill went wild with fruit. The bird was happy.

Later, it nestled into a warm hollow near the base of the tree.

"For when you need your rest," it said.
